name: seaborn
tagline: Reference guide for seaborn statistical plotting in Python — plot types, palettes, theming, and common analysis patterns.
verdict: Well-scoped, technically solid reference skill: pinned install commands, clear negative triggers to plotly/scientific-visualization, concrete runnable examples throughout, and a version field. Main gaps: three referenced files (function_reference.md, objects_interface.md, examples.md) don't resolve in the provided bundle, and SKILL.md runs past the size.

highlights.summary: 711-line SKILL.md inlines full per-category parameter catalogs (e.g. line 107) duplicating what references/function_reference.md is meant to hold.
Lines 705-707 point to references/function_reference.md, objects_interface.md, examples.md — none present in the provided skill content.
SKILL.md is 711 lines, about 1.4x the ~500-line budget, though under the 25KB byte ceiling.
